title: anthyllis montana subsp. hispanica
intro: Anthyllis montana subsp. hispanica, also known as Spanish kidney vetch or mountain kidney vetch, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its pink or white flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of anthyllis montana subsp. hispanica include relief from urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and bladder infections, as well as potential benefits for digestive problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of anthyllis montana subsp. hispanica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal compounds and promote overall well-being.
